qa_pairs.question: What command is used to determine the current Access Control List (ACL) attributes for a directory like `/project/g/group/[owner]`?
qa_pairs.answer: To determine the current ACL attributes for a directory such as `/project/g/group/[owner]`, you would use the command `$ mmgetacl /project/g/group/[owner]`.

qa_pairs.question: How can a user remove a previously configured Access Control List (ACL) from a directory on a SciNet system?
qa_pairs.answer: To remove any previously set Access Control List (ACL) from a directory, for example `/project/g/group/[owner]`, you can use the command `$ mmdelacl -d /project/g/group/[owner]`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the command to create or modify a GPFS Access Control List (ACL) for a specific directory?
qa_pairs.answer: You can create or change a GPFS Access Control List (ACL) for a directory, such as `/project/g/group/[owner]`, by using the command `$ mmeditacl /project/g/group/[owner]`.
